title: The Core Idea of Attention
concept: Attention shifts modeling from 'remember everything' to 'look at what matters right now', paving the way for advanced NLP. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: NLP Basics Capstone.'

exercises.prompt: What major issue did early language models face when processing long paragraphs?

options.text: Forgetting the beginning of long sentences

exercises.explain: Older models suffered from "forgetfulness" with long inputs. Attention solves this by pointing a spotlight at the most relevant words.

cards.title: Stop Forgetting Everything!
cards.body: Older AI models had a terrible habit: if you gave them a long sentence, they would forget the beginning by the time they reached the end! Attention changed everything by letting AI 'look back' at what matters.

cards.title: What is Attention?
cards.body: Attention is a mechanism in AI that allows a model to focus on specific parts of the input sequence when generating an output, rather than trying to compress the entire sequence into a single fixed summary.

cards.title: The Book Reader Analogy
cards.body: Imagine writing a book report. Instead of memorizing the entire 500-page book word-for-word, you skim the chapters and zoom in on the exact paragraphs that answer your question. Attention does the same thing: it scans the whole input but zooms in on the most useful parts.

cards.title: Attention in Translation
cards.body: When translating the French sentence "Le chat noir" to the English "The black cat", the AI needs to translate "chat" to "cat". The Attention mechanism tells the AI to look closely at "chat" (cat) while translating, even if the word order changes.

cards.title: Myth: AI is Conscious Now
cards.body: People think Attention makes an AI 'think' exactly like a human brain. In reality, it is just a math technique that calculates weight percentages for words to see which ones pair up best.

cards.title: Compression vs. Spotlight
cards.body: See how the core approach of AI changed with the invention of the Attention mechanism.

left.text: Try to pack the meaning of a whole 100-word sentence into one tight suitcase. Details get lost!

right.text: Keep the whole sentence open and shine a spotlight directly on the words needed at this exact millisecond.

exercises.prompt: What was the main limitation of older AI models before the Attention mechanism?

options.text: They were too fast to read anything.
They would forget the beginning of long sentences.
They only worked with numbers, not words.
They could only translate one word per day.

exercises.explain: Before Attention, AI tried to squeeze an entire sentence's meaning into one small, fixed-size mathematical summary, which often caused it to forget details in long sentences.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Attention mechanism forces the AI to only look at the very last word of a sentence.
exercises.explain: Attention allows the AI to dynamically look back at any part of the input sentence, no matter how long it is.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the core goal of the Attention mechanism.
exercises.sentence: Instead of memorizing everything, the Attention mechanism helps the AI to [blank] on what matters right now.
exercises.explain: The Attention mechanism allows AI to selectively focus on the most important words in a sentence.

exercises.prompt: Which AI system is using an Attention mechanism to process the sentence: "The dog ran because it was scared"?

options.text: System A: It connects the word 'it' back to 'dog' by analyzing which words have the strongest relationship.
System B: It counts the total number of letters and decides the sentence is 31 characters long.
System C: It translates the words one-by-one in order without checking how they relate to each other.

exercises.explain: System A uses Attention because it actively looks at and links the pronoun 'it' to the specific word 'dog' to understand what ran away.

exercises.prompt: How does the Attention mechanism solve the problem of processing long texts in AI?

options.text: It memorizes every word in a database forever.
It processes only the very first word of a book.
It dynamically weights and focuses on the most relevant parts of the input.
It randomly deletes half of the sentence to save space.

exercises.explain: By focusing only on the relevant words, Attention avoids forgetting long-term details and makes AI translations and summaries incredibly accurate. Next up: NLP Basics Capstone.
